clusters.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
clusters.neutral_headline: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary prime minister

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era
items.standfirst: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...
items.content: Jubilation in Budapest as new leader invites people to ‘step through gate of regime change’The pro-European centre-right leader Péter Magyar has been sworn in as prime minister of Hungary, marking the official end to Viktor Orbán’s 16 years in power.Saturday’s ceremony – during which Magyar had invited people to join him to “write Hungarian history” together and “step through the gate of regime change” – comes a month after his opposition Tisza party won a landslide victory in parliamentary elections. Continue reading...

items.title: Péter Magyar Takes Office, Marking the Official End of the Orbán Era
items.standfirst: Peter Magyar has officially been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, almost a month after defeating Viktor Orban.
items.content: Peter Magyar has officially been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, almost a month after defeating Viktor Orban.

items.title: I will serve - not rule over Hungary, says new PM
items.standfirst: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.
items.content: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

items.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary's prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's rule
items.standfirst: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.
items.content: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party defeated Orbán's nationalist-populist Fidesz in a stunning blow last month.

items.title: Hungary’s new prime minister sworn in after landslide defeat of Viktor Orban
items.standfirst: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.
items.content: Centre-right leader Peter Magyar has been sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister.

items.title: Peter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s PM, ending Orban’s 16 years in power
items.standfirst: Magyar's Tisza party secured a huge parliamentary majority in last month's election.
items.content: Magyar's Tisza party secured a huge parliamentary majority in last month's election.

items.title: 'Clean break' with Orban: Hungary's Magyar to usher in 'new era'
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.

items.title: Magyar to succeed Orban as Hungary's new prime minister
items.standfirst: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.
items.content: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar set to be sworn in as new prime minister
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.

items.title: Magyar sworn in as prime minister as Hungary turns the page on Orban
items.standfirst: Hungary’s parliament was set to swear in opposition leader Peter Magyar as prime minister on Saturday, marking the end of Viktor Orban’s 16 years in power after the pro-European conservative challenger won a landslide election victory on promises to tackle corruption and restore democratic institutions.
items.content: Hungary’s parliament was set to swear in opposition leader Peter Magyar as prime minister on Saturday, marking the end of Viktor Orban’s 16 years in power after the pro-European conservative challenger won a landslide election victory on promises to tackle corruption and restore democratic institutions.

Centre right leader marks new era for Hungary

clusters.title: Details from the Pentagon's release of dozens of UFO files

items.title: Details from the Pentagon's release of dozens of UFO files
items.standfirst: The Pentagon began releasing more files Friday on unidentified flying objects and unidentified anomalous phenomena after an order from President Trump.
items.content: The Pentagon began releasing more files Friday on unidentified flying objects and unidentified anomalous phenomena after an order from President Trump.

items.title: Pentagon releases new files on UFOs
items.standfirst: Bright lights and mysterious objects, those are what could be found in a new batch of files on UFOs that the Pentagon began releasing on Friday as President Donald Trump taps into the public's long-held curiosities about "unidentified anomalous phenomena” in the broader universe.

clusters.title: Russia and Ukraine begin 3-day ceasefire as tensions ramp up between U.S. and Iran

items.title: Russia and Ukraine begin 3-day ceasefire as tensions ramp up between U.S. and Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days. Meanwhile, the U.S. is awaiting Iran's response to a peace proposal.
items.content: President Trump said Friday that fighting would halt between Russia and Ukraine for three days. Meanwhile, the U.S. is awaiting Iran's response to a peace proposal.

items.title: Three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced
items.standfirst: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.
items.content: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.

items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged Saturday to revive the Labour Party after a disastrous set of local and regional elections.

items.title: Hard-right party routs Starmer’s Labour in U.K. elections as Trump ally hails ‘historic shift’
items.standfirst: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.
items.content: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.
